title: Functions, Transformations, Inverses, and Composition · Worked Case: Composition
summary: Model sensor calibration and reversible conversions across representations. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int composition increment in a calibration and conversion library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compose functions while checking intermediate domain and unit compatibility.
Construct and validate inverses under one-to-one domain restrictions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Composition evidence
steps.instruction: A model passes through (3, 11) and (7, 23). Calculate its average rate of change. Use the result to decide Composition for a calibration and conversion library.
steps.why: The Composition result for a calibration and conversion library is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (23 − 11) / (7 − 3). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compose functions while checking intermediate domain and unit compatibility.
